title: Richly Spiced Middle Eastern Eggs Shakshuka
description: A briny and flavorful Middle Eastern dish featuring tamarind pulp, perfect for a date night, slow cooked in a tagine pot to create a glassy jelly texture, following ayurvedic rajasic friendly principles in the form of shakshuka.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the tagine pot over medium low heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for one minute.
Add the tamarind pulp and cook for two minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the canned crushed tomatoes and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for twenty minutes, stirring occasionally.
Create six wells in the tomato mixture and crack an egg into each well.
Cover the tagine pot and cook for an additional ten to fifteen minutes, or until the eggs are cooked to the desired doneness.
Serve the shakshuka hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
